title: Fast fashion drove Bangladesh - now its troubled economy ...Bangladesh's Garments Revolution: New Report Challenges ...Expanding and Diversifying Exports in BangladeshWhat’s happening in Bangladesh’s garment industry?
snippet: Sep 4, 2024 ·Fast fashion droveBangladesh- now its troubled economy needs more Many garment manufacturing jobs remain well below the minimum wage Nikhil Inamdar Business correspondent in DhakaMay 12, 2025 ·The report, Beyond the Stereotype: RethinkingBangladesh's Textile Industry, highlightsBangladesh's emergence as a leader in ethical and sustainable apparel production.In the context ofBangladesh’s upcoming LDC graduation in2026and the current economic challenges, there is a growing realization thatpolicyreforms to enhance export competitiveness might receive stronger domestic support than in the past.Does Bangladesh have a ready-made garment industry?In particular,the ready-made garment (RMG) sector occupies an outsized position in Bangladesh’s economy– and by extension, in the national imagination. Widely regarded as the engine of development, the rise of the RMG industry was meteoric. In 1983-84, apparel accounted for less than 4% of total Bangladeshi exports.What is Bangladesh's role in the textile and clothing industry?The textile and clothing industry is one themain sources of Bangladesh’s recent economic growth and development. In particular, the ready-made garment (RMG) sector occupies an outsized position in Bangladesh’s economy – and by extension, in the national imagination.Does Bangladesh offer duty-free market access to Asian LDCs?As an LDC, Bangladesh enjoys unilateral trade preferences in Canada, the UK, and the EU, with the US market being the only notable exception,not offering duty-free market access to Asian LDCs. This duty-free market access, in stark contrast to the high MFN tariffs, has endowed Bangladesh with a significant competitive edge.What are the challenges faced by Bangladesh's trade policy regime?The challenges identified in these various sources fall into two main categories, i.e., the trade policy regime in Bangladeshdiscriminates against exports, and weak trade infrastructure and other unfavorable business-enabling factors undermine export competitiveness.Mar 26, 2025 ·Bangladeshis well-known for its low-cost production of textiles and clothing, with much of the country’s recent development resting on rapid growth in the garment industry. But behind this progress is an unsustainable reliance on low pay and poor working conditions, particularly for women.
